sentences.S1: At what input-precision level does aggregate-data reproduction of fixed-effects 2-trial pools become tolerance-limited by rounding alone, independent of implementation correctness?
sentences.S2: Ten thousand Monte Carlo 2-trial pools: true log-HR uniform over [log 0.6, log 1.3], per-trial SE uniform [0.04, 0.08], xoshiro128** seed 20260415.
sentences.S3: Each pool rounded at dp in {1,2,3,4} decimal places on both HR and CI bounds, re-pooled via fixed-effects inverse-variance on log-HR, compared to the unrounded pool.
sentences.S5: Floor scales monotonically with 10⁻ᵈᵖ across all four levels; both median and 95th percentile fall by approximately a decade per dp increment.
sentences.S6: Benchmark-reproduction tolerance must exceed the precision floor at the input dp; δ=0.005 at dp=2 lies above the 0.0044 p95 yet below max 0.0084, explaining the prior SGLT2i FAIL as a tail outcome, not a defect.
sentences.S7: Scope: k=2, fixed-effects, unimodal true-HR distribution; random-effects, k>2, and IPD precision floors remain open questions.
